title: Shahid Minar
shortDescription: A handmade interpretation of the Shaheed Minar, capturing the symbolic vertical pillars and central red disc that represent sacrifice and language identity. The structure is carefully assembled using layered board to create depth and symmetry, while maintaining a clean, minimal palette. The spacing between the columns reflects the original monument’s rhythm and openness. Small detailing at the base adds a personal, crafted touch without overpowering the form. Built as both a tribute and a study of cultural symbolism through physical form.

title: National Memorial
shortDescription: This piece recreates the geometric rise of the National Martyrs’ Memorial using repeated angled panels to mimic its iconic converging structure. The layered construction gives a sense of scale and perspective despite the compact size. The addition of the flag introduces motion and national context, grounding the abstract form in identity. The base is kept simple to emphasize the upward movement of the monument. It functions as a balance between architectural accuracy and handmade interpretation.

title: Palki (Palanqueen)
shortDescription: A handmade palanquin inspired by traditional Bengali wedding processions, where the bride would be carried with music, lights, and celebration. The structure reflects that sense of movement and festivity, with extended poles and a central chamber decorated in vibrant patterns. The colors and motifs draw from folk art styles often seen in rural celebrations. It’s built to feel lively and ceremonial rather than perfectly precise. The piece captures a moment of joy, tradition, and community gathering.

title: Nagardola (Ferris Wheel)
shortDescription: A small handcrafted nagordola inspired by the village fairs and melas that are a familiar part of Bengali life. The rotating structure and hanging seats reflect the simple rides often set up during festivals like Pohela Boishakh. Bright decorative patterns give it a playful, festive feel, echoing the colors and energy of those gatherings. Even without motion, it carries the sense of excitement and nostalgia tied to childhood fair experiences.
